frameworks.name: reference-dependent valuation
frameworks.one_line: Options are evaluated relative to a hidden internal benchmark.

frameworks.name: distributed population coding
frameworks.one_line: Reference and subjective-value signals are distributed across frontal regions.

core_question: Where and how is the decisional reference point encoded in primate frontal cortex?
key_assumptions: A hidden reference point underlies subjective valuation
Reference point can be dissociated from objective reward via wealth accumulation
Population activity reveals encoding not visible at single-neuron level
manipulations: Wealth accumulation task dissociating objective value from reference
Recording across six frontal regions including vbACC, dACC, dlPFC
key_findings: Reference-related signals were broadly distributed at single-neuron level across frontal regions
At population level, only vbACC significantly encoded the reference point
dACC and dlPFC encoded reference-dependent subjective value at the population level
Temporal sequence and anatomy suggest vbACC supplies reference signal to downstream value regions
interpretations: A dedicated frontal circuit underlies reference dependence, with vbACC as a global reference source
Reference encoding and reference-dependent value encoding are anatomically separable
